title: Incorrect Count of Leading Ones in Binary Representation

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

hints.text: Check the return statement in the function.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int countLeadingOnes(int n) { int ones = 0; while (n) { if (n & 1) ones++; n >>= 1; } return ones == 32 ? 32 : ones; } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public static int countLeadingOnes(int n) { int ones = 0; while (n != 0) { if (n & 1) ones++; n >>= 1; } return ones == 32 ? 32 : ones; } }
starterCode.javascript: function countLeadingOnes(n) { let ones = 0; while (n) { if (n & 1) ones++; n >>= 1; } return ones === 32 ? 32 : ones; }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int countLeadingOnes(int n) { int ones = 0; while (n) { if (n & 1) ones++; n >>= 1; } return ones == 32 ? 32 : ones+1; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public static int countLeadingOnes(int n) { int ones = 0; while (n != 0) { if (n & 1) ones++; n >>= 1; } return ones == 32 ? 32 : ones+1; } }
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function countLeadingOnes(n) { let ones = 0; while (n) { if (n & 1) ones++; n >>= 1; } return ones === 32 ? 32 : ones+1; }
